title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · SQL Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a guarded ticket-mutation workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.
Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Insert Values Select
steps.instruction:
Order the Insert Values Select evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Insert Values Select.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Insert Values Select assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Insert Values Select, keep Insert Values Select connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Import ticket 202 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.
